data.title: Peter Schiff warns of bear market for Bitcoin treasury companies like Michael Saylor's, predicts 45% decline in MSTR stock.
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff has warned of a looming bear market for companies following Michael Saylor-led Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR) in their Bitcoin treasury plays. Schiff criticized Saylor's strategy, pointing out that MSTR is down 45% from its peak in November 2024. Schiff predicted a harsh bear market for Bitcoin treasury companies and expressed doubts about their survival. He highlighted the potential struggles ahead for companies like MSTR. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Optical transceiver market to hit $36.73B by 2031, with a CAGR of 14.2%, driven by rising data traffic and bandwidth demand.
data.description: In a recent report by The Insight Partners, the optical transceiver market was described as experiencing significant growth due to various factors. These include the rising volume of data traffic, a growing demand for higher bandwidth, a shift towards cloud-based services, and an increasing requirement for high-speed connections. The market is thriving with substantial momentum, showcasing promising opportunities for growth and development.

data.title: World's first commercially available humanoid robot, K2 "Bumblebee," by Kepler Robotics, begins mass production in Shanghai.
data.description: In Shanghai on September 26, 2025, Kepler Robotics Co., Ltd revealed the commencement of mass production for its K2 "Bumblebee" model. This innovation marks the world's pioneer commercially accessible hybrid-architecture humanoid. The announcement was made public via a video release.

data.title: Soligenix priced $7.5 million public offering to develop rare disease treatments. Company focused on unmet medical needs.
data.description: In Princeton, New Jersey on September 26, 2025, Soligenix, Inc. (Nasdaq: SNGX) revealed the pricing details for their products. The company, a late-stage biopharmaceutical firm, specializes in creating and distributing treatments for rare diseases with unmet medical requirements.

data.title: Weekly tech news recap: 11 press releases including Compass and Anywhere's merger, Amplify launch, and banking's AI report.
data.description: This week saw significant tech developments as reported by PR Newswire. The merger of Compass and Anywhere captured attention, signaling a major shift in the industry. Additionally, PR Newswire introduced the Amplify platform, offering a new media distribution service. A noteworthy report on the future of AI in banking also emerged. The events unfolded in New York on September 26, 2025, with a plethora of press releases making waves across the industry.

data.title: Bio-Techne introduces RNAscope™ and Lunaphore COMET™ for simultaneous RNA and protein detection, enhancing spatial biology solutions for translational research.
data.description: The combination of RNAscope™ technology and the Lunaphore COMET™ allows for the concurrent detection of RNA and protein on a single tissue section. This innovative approach supports spatial multiomic analysis, providing valuable insights for pathology workflows. Research indicates the potential for improved clinical utility workflows, particularly for lymphoid cases. This technology offers enhanced capabilities for examining biological samples, aimed at optimizing diagnostic processes. The integration of these technologies showcases promising prospects for more comprehensive and detailed analysis in the medical field.

data.title: KinderFarms introduces KinderMed Pain & Fever medicine for infants & children, offering a cleaner option.
data.description: Today, in Los Angeles on September 26, 2025, KinderFarms unveiled their latest product, KinderMed Pain & Fever, as part of their mission to provide gentle and safe healthcare solutions for families. This new over-the-counter medication is specifically designed for infants and children, catering to their unique needs. KinderMed Pain & Fever aims to address common symptoms in children, offering a kinder alternative for parents.

data.title: Eason Technology Ltd filed Form 20-F for FY 2024, regaining NYSE American compliance.Characters: 74
data.description: In Hong Kong on September 26, 2025, Eason Technology Limited (NYSE American: DXF), a company involved in real estate operation management, investment, and digital technology security, disclosed the submission of its yearly report.

data.title: Ethereum shows 'rare oversold signal' for first time since $1.4K, indicating potential bottom as relative strength index dips.
data.description: The decline in Ethereum's price in the last two weeks has led to the relative strength index indicating a possible bottom signal that has not been observed since early April.

data.title: Weekly recap of 14 health press releases, including women's health awareness & new funding for breast cancer research.
data.description: This week, a summary of key healthcare announcements on PR Newswire includes highlights on women's health initiatives, with an emphasis on raising awareness. Notably, a significant amount of new funding has been allocated towards breast cancer research. Additionally, the healthcare sector is witnessing advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) technology, which is expected to revolutionize various facets of medical services. The updates come amidst the publication of numerous press releases, indicating a high level of activity within the industry.

data.title: NVIDIA reveals 3 new neural technologies for robot learning, closing gap between simulation and real-world use.
data.description: NVIDIA has revealed three revolutionary neural technologies designed to improve robot learning and close the divide between simulation and real-world use. The company's latest innovations mark a significant step forward in the field, with the aim of boosting the capabilities of robots. These advancements showcase NVIDIA's commitment to advancing technology and pushing the boundaries of what is possible in the realm of robotics.

data.title: Costco, Concentrix featured in the news. Heritage Financial to acquire Olympic Bancorp for $176.6 million. Inventiva expected to report revenue of $5.31 million.
data.description: Today, U.S. stock futures are trading with a mix, drawing investor focus towards several key stocks. Heritage Financial Corp (NASDAQ: HFWA) intends to acquire Olympic Bancorp in a significant all-stock deal valued at $176.6 million. Heritage Financial's shares closed at $24.60 on Thursday, experiencing a 0.2% drop. Expectations from Wall Street predict that Inventiva S.A. (NASDAQ: IVA) will announce a loss of 41 cents per share alongside revenue of $5.31 million for the first half of the year. This news comes ahead of the market open today. To find out more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: US stocks fall for third day, Nasdaq drops 100+ points. Fear & Greed index in 'Greed' zone. Economic news: US durable goods orders rise 2.9% in August.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index indicated a decrease in market sentiment, remaining in the "Greed" zone. The U.S. stock market experienced a decline on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping over 100 points. The S&P 500 also fell for the third consecutive session. Accenture (NYSE: ACN) reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results for 2025. Conversely, CarMax, Inc. (NYSE: KMX) saw a 20% decrease in shares after reporting second-quarter EPS and sales below projections. U.S. durable goods orders rose by 2.9% month-over-month to $312.1 billion in August, surpassing market estimates of a 0.5% increase. The July data was revised to show a 2.7% decline. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: AL_SEASON showcases 26 Korean craft artists with ADÈLE Collections in Paris, running through Sept. 30.
data.description: In Paris, the event "Gyeol – Where the Light Lingers" by AL_SEASON features ADÈLE Collections, displaying the works of 26 Korean craft artists until September 30th.
